sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: (P.\,Hall). Must a non-trivial group, which is isomorphic to each of its non-trivial normal subgroups, be either free of infinite rank, simple, or infinite cyclic? (Lennox, Smith and Wiegold, 1992, have shown that a finitely generated group of this kind which has a proper normal subgroup of finite index is infinite cyclic.) { J.\,C.\,Lennox} No (V.\,N.\,Obraztsov, Proc. London Math. Soc., { 75} (1...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
